items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

image.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

items.title: Motorcyclist's family urges court to jail woman who failed to give way

children.content: The family of a beloved member of the Northern Territory's motorsport community has urged a court to send a woman who failed to give way to him to jail over the fatal crash.

image.altText: Two women embrace outside court.

items.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

articles.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A woman sitting at the bar inside a dim hotel, looking over the mementos plastered on the walls around her.

thumbnail.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO

items.title: Ex-cop turned missing persons expert may know where Peter Falconio is

children.content: Jim Whitehead has helped to close some of Australia's most high-profile missing persons cases, including those of Daniel Morcombe and Allison Baden-Clay. Now he is looking to help find the remains of British backpacker Peter Falconio.

image.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

items.title: Why are the world's biggest AI firms so attracted to the NT?

children.content: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

image.altText: A computer-generated image of a huge industrial site in an outback area.

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say

children.content: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels

children.content: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

Women's feet in high heels, under red lights.

thumbnail.altText: The black silhouette of a girl, with blurred lights in the background.

items.title: Batteries charge ahead as data centres fuel gas turbine costs: CSIRO

children.content: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

image.altText: Flames belch form two tall metal industrial chimneys.

items.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework

children.content: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit with a coat, a red tie and glasses.

items.title: What Australia's economy could look like under One Nation

children.content: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson looks to the side seriously.

items.title: As Japan undergoes a defence shift, China warns it's 'becoming a powder keg'

children.content: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the midst of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

image.altText: Japanese naval officers look over a ship.

items.title: Buyers 'back in the driver's seat' in south-east Queensland as property market slows

children.content: After a decades-long
real estate boom, experts say the balance of power is finally shifting from sellers to buyers.

thumbnail.altText: A young lady with shoulder-length brown hair standing in a driveway poses for camera. She is wearing a brown jacket.

items.title: Smart glasses help vision-impaired despite privacy concerns

children.content: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

image.altText: A close up photo of Sam's face wearing his black AI glasses.

items.title: Suspected Widdecombe murder being treated as a targeted attack

children.content: After initially saying the alleged murder of British former politician Ann Widdecombe was not politically motivated, police now say they believe it was a targeted attack.

image.altText: An older woman wearing a light-blue rosette points as she speaks into a microphone in front of a Reform UK logo.

items.title: No fee, no sign-in: The art class removing 'barriers' to recovery

children.content: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

gallery.altText: A woman sits at a table drawing a picture with pencils.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits at a table drawing a picture with pencils.

items.title: Woman sues ex-Catholic archbishop over alleged abuse by another priest

children.content: A Tasmanian woman is suing former Archbishop of Hobart Julian Porteous and the state of Tasmania over allegations of sexual abuse by two others when she was a child.

image.altText: Julian Porteous stands in front of a full church.

items.title: Monet, Renoir, van Gogh masterpieces appear in two new blockbuster exhibitions

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

image.altText: Three paintings hung in a gallery with red walls.

items.title: Quiz: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip

children.content: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

items.title: 'I'm ruling it out': Taylor categorically kills off One Nation coalition talk

children.content: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with it.

image.altText: A man in suit and tie smiling while sitting at a desk.

items.title: 'People want community': Hobby-based clubs tackling loneliness

children.content: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.

items.title: Fertiliser company fined after hundreds of complaints about manure stench

children.content: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

image.altText: Piles of fertilisers and dirt in rows on a large piece of land containing several sheds on the side

items.title: Concerns over loophole allowing residents to shut down Victorian councils

children.content: Advocates say that unproven charges such as those against councillors at Hepburn Shire, triggering automatic stand-down laws, can bring any other council across the state to a shuddering halt.

image.altText: A council office

items.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon outlines plans for pine, cattle and tourism

children.content: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

items.title: Aussie investors fear millions in losses as alleged fake bond scheme unravels

children.content: The financial watchdog is seeking to shut down an investment company accused of taking $17 million from 80 victims, including by promoting fake bonds.

image.altText: Gail MacDonald, a 69-year-old woman from Wollongong, is a a Capital Guard victim.

items.title: 'They give us more power': Pogačar wins Tour stage, dismisses booing fans

children.content: Tadej Pogačar takes another step towards a fifth Tour de France win with a breakaway stage victory, with some fans booing him because of his dominance.

image.altText: The race leader in the Tour de France, wearing yellow, smiles and sits up over the line as he wins a stage.

items.title: Spy agency warns active Russian hackers targeting Australian industry

children.content: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

gallery.altText: Cybersecurity experts say the longer a breach goes undetected, the more time attackers have to steal victims' data.

thumbnail.altText: Cybersecurity experts say the longer a breach goes undetected, the more time attackers have to steal victims' data.

items.title: Revelations about Bill Gates's ties to Epstein prompts charity investor to exit

children.content: The American investor will end his longtime donations to the Microsoft co-founder's non-profit.

image.altText: Warren Buffett wearing a navy jacket and red tie speaking while sitting in a black chair and gesturing with his left hand

items.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand

children.content: Tasmania's top health official says the Launceston General Hospital is experiencing "impacted" capacity and is urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

image.altText: Exteriors of a public hospital.

items.title: What we know about the alleged murder of Jana Armstrong

children.content: The search for Toowoomba mother Jana Armstrong ended in tragedy. Here's what police say happened to the young mother.

items.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations

children.content: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

items.title: The worrying statistic around Indigenous players in the AFLW

children.content: Racism remains a key issue for First Nations AFL players, while Indigenous AFLW players rate their workplace culture significantly lower than their peers, a new report finds.

image.altText: Indigenous round signage is seen before the round seven AFLW match.

items.title: Jack announces swimming retirement on eve of Commonwealth Games

children.content: Gold medal-winning Australian swimmer Shayna Jack says she will retire at the end of the Commonwealth Games.

image.altText: Shayna Jack in the pool after a race, smiling with her goggles on her head

items.title: NSW scrapped this 'frustrating' road rule. Should other jurisdictions follow?

children.content: The NRMA is encouraging all states and territories to follow the lead of NSW in removing reduced speed limits for roadworks outside construction hours.

image.altText: A temporary sign saying Roadwork Ahead and a 40 kph speed limit.

items.title: Sam Neill on his career, dying and the role he turned down

children.content: Looking back at the wisdom and insight of the beloved New Zealand actor.

articles.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

image.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.

articles.title: Sam Neill leaves a wealth of films behind. Here are some of his best

children.content: With more than 150 acting roles under his belt, Sam Neill leaves a lasting legacy to the film and TV industry, and an array of characters that will stay in fans' hearts forever.
